stories.title: Lithuania lifts air alert after suspected drones approaching from Belarus diverted - Europe live
stories.summary: Incident comes just a day after Nato had to shoot down a suspected stray Ukrainian drone over Estonia In Brussels, Rutte begins by stressing the importance of Nato ministers meeting in Sweden, the alliance’s “newest ally” who joined in 2024. “This says a great deal about how fundamentally our security environment has changed, especially when it comes to Europe. It is more dangerous, it is more contested and it makes it all the more important that Nato allies work together to safeguard our freedo

stories.title: Defense department watchdog opens inquiry into US airstrikes on alleged drug boats
stories.summary: Officials will determine if standard process was followed before lethal strikes in Caribbean and eastern Pacific Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The Pentagon’s internal watchdog has opened an investigation into whether US military commanders followed proper procedures when conducting boat strikes in the Caribbean and the eastern Pacific. The office of inspector general at the Department of Defense is examining whether military commanders stuck to the standard six-step process t

stories.title: Georgia mayor who fired town’s entire police force resigns, citing family ‘health concerns’
stories.summary: Ron Shinnick did not mention the firing of the Cohutta police force, which was later rehired, in resignation letter The mayor of a small town in the US state of Georgia has resigned shortly after firing his community’s entire police department, a step that the local governing council ultimately reversed – but that he nonetheless took amid a political spat pitting him and his wife against members of the force. In a 15 May resignation letter that the Guardian reviewed, Ron Shinnick avoided mention

stories.title: NATO planes down suspected Ukrainian drone over Estonia
stories.summary: A NATO military jet has shot down a suspected Ukrainian drone in Estonian airspace. As Ukraine targets Russia's Baltic oil facilities more often and more intensely, more such drones are straying into allies' territory.

stories.title: Arsenal win the Premier League ending 22-year wait after Manchester City draw with Bournemouth
stories.summary: The Gunners have finally secured a Premier League title ending a 22-year wait for the coveted title after Manchester City drew with Bournemouth. The north Londoners can now set their sights on winning their first Champions League final in Budapest, where they face holders Paris Saint-Germain.

stories.title: Russian jamming blamed after Nato jet downs Ukrainian drone over Estonia
stories.summary: Officials from Baltic states say Moscow behind latest such incident but also tell Kyiv to be more careful with its routing A Romanian F-16 Nato jet shot down a drone over Estonia on Tuesday in what appears to be the latest case of Russian electronic jamming diverting long-range Ukrainian drones into the alliance’s territory. A local resident told the Estonian public broadcaster, ERR, that he had seen two fighter jets – part of a Nato force policing the skies over the Baltic states – flying in th

stories.title: Three Toronto police officers arrested over sexual assault in Barcelona
stories.summary: Two off-duty officers allegedly assaulted a sex worker in a taxi in Ciutat Vella, according to police in Catalonia Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The Toronto police force, which is already under intense public scrutiny, is facing fresh questions after it emerged that three off-duty officers on vacation in Barcelona were arrested in connection with a sexual assault last week. According to police in Barcelona, the alleged assault occurred in the early hours of 13 May, when the t

stories.title: US warns Russia after Moscow threatens Latvia – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed, follow the latest development on today’s Europe blog The latest drone alerts come as Ukraine and Latvia were this morning forced by Russia to repeatedly refute Moscow’s claims that Kyiv was preparing attacks against Russia from Latvia. Ukraine’s foreign ministry spokesperson confirmed that Ukraine does not use the territory of Latvia for its operations against Russia and refuted Moscow’s claims. “ Russia is lying about Latvia allowing any country to use Latvian airspace

stories.title: Czechia: Uproar over Sudeten German gathering in Brno
stories.summary: The first gathering of Sudeten Germans in Czechia since World War II is set to go ahead this weekend despite a political backlash that has exposed tensions over one of Central Europe's most painful historical legacies.

stories.title: Hungary's new Prime Minister Magyar seeks to fix Poland ties
stories.summary: Peter Magyar hopes to mend relations with Poland during his first state visit to Warsaw. What lessons could he learn from Poland's Prime Minister Donald Tusk?

stories.title: Estonia says Nato jet shot down drone over its territory
stories.summary: Estonia suspects the drone was a Ukrainian projectile knocked off course by Russian electronic jamming.

stories.title: A MÁV minden negyedik vonatát leállítva hagyta hátra Lázár János
stories.summary: A volt közlekedési miniszter azt ígérte, hogy száz leállított InterCity-kocsit ismét üzembe állítanak, de még kevesebb lett a használható kocsi, és a dízel motorvonatoknál is rengeteg a probléma.

stories.title: A klub, ami többször nyerte meg a BEK-et, mint a saját országa bajnokságát
stories.summary: A Nottingham Forest csodát tett: újoncként lett bajnok Angliában, majd rögtön BEK-győztes, és még meg is védte a címet. A hazai bajnoki címet soha többé nem érték el.

stories.title: Kőszegen újabb helyeken mutattak ki azbesztszennyezést, az osztrák rohonci bánya is érintett lehet
stories.summary: Újabb több tucat mintában mutattak ki szennyezést, minden vizsgált esetben pozitív lett az eredmény.

stories.title: Magyar Péter és Donald Tusk nemzetközi sajtótájékoztatója – élőben a Tisza-kormány első napjairól
stories.summary: Ma van az 1% határideje, segítsd te is a munkánkat! Villámgyors kormányalakítás után Magyar Péter és kormánya bombázni kezdte a NER-t, de mi történik a szimbolikus ügyeken túl? Félnapos adásban a Telex újságíróival mutatjuk be a Tisza-kormány első napjait, és élőben kapcsoljuk Magyar Péter és Donald Tusk sajtótájékoztatóját.

stories.title: Heteken belül fizetni kell majd a Revolutról külső számlára indított, 50 ezer forint feletti utalásokért
stories.summary: A Revolut áthelyezi a magyarországi ügyfeleit a magyar fióktelepéhez.

stories.title: GKI: A kormány előtt álló egyik legfontosabb kihívás az uniós rangsor végén kullogó magyar bérek felzárkóztatása
stories.summary: Az elmúlt 16 év jelentős reálbéremelkedése nem volt elég a bérfelzárkóztatáshoz, a folytatás előtt pedig komoly akadályok állnak.

stories.title: Orbán Anita megnyitotta a magyar–ukrán egyeztetést
stories.summary: Orbán Anita Andrij Szibiha ukrán külügyminiszterrel és Tarasz Kacska ukrán miniszterelnök-helyettessel nyitotta meg az online egyeztetést.

stories.summary: Kürti Gábor, a Magyar Kerékpárosklub elnöke valószínűleg az első városi kajakos, aki végigment a Rákos-patak 300 méter hosszú alagútján.

stories.title: Consultations between Ukraine and Hungary officially underway
stories.summary: The consultations were opened by Ukrainian MFA Andrii Sybiha, Deputy PM Taras Kachka and Hungarian MFA, Anita Orbán.

stories.summary: Keressük a kampányidőszak legbizarrabb történetét, a legunalmasabbak már lemorzsolódtak, innentől repülőrajt! Melyik volt furább: Orbán alakításai vagy az agyrohasztó mesterséges intelligenciás tartalmak?

stories.title: Katonai tiszteletadással fogadták Magyar Pétert és kormánytagokat Varsóban, elkezdődtek a tárgyalások
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök koszorúzással kezdte a napot, ahonnan a lengyel kormányfő rezidenciájára mentek.

stories.title: Másfél év után újra járni fognak a vonatok Veszprém és Ajka között
stories.summary: 2024. decemberben azért kellett lezárni egy 150 méteres szakaszt, mert egy spórolós megoldás miatt nem bírta a vízalámosást a pálya.

stories.title: Kifli.hu: fókuszban a helyi termelők és a hazai vásárlók (x)
stories.summary: Budapest, 2026. május 19. – A népszerű online szupermarket a következő években a magyar piacra szabott működésre, a hazai termelői együttműködések erősítésére és a friss, helyi termékek kínálatának bővítésére helyezi a hangsúlyt. A vállalat keddi sajtótájékoztatóján elhangzottak szerint az online-kereskedelem jövője nem pusztán a gyorsaságról és a technológiáról szól, hanem egyre inkább a bizalomról, az átláthatóságról és a helyi értékekről.

stories.title: Donald Tusk Magyar Péternek: Mindannyian nagyon meg vagyunk itt hatódva Lengyelországban
stories.summary: Magyar Péter első útja kormányfőként Varsóba vezetett, tárgyalásuk után sajtótájékoztatót tartanak a lengyel miniszterelnökkel. Utána Magyar a PiS-hez köthető Nawrocki elnökkel is találkozik.

stories.title: Egyértelmű az irányváltás, de a gyors elszámoltatás és vitatott döntések teljes visszafordítása akadozott
stories.summary: Magyar Péter Lengyelországba érkezett első hivatalos kormányfői útjára. A Fideszhez hasonlóan Brüsszel ellen hadakozó PiS-t legyőző Tusk a vártnál lassabban halad a jogállamiságot megerősítő reformokkal, mint azt forrásokat részben megnyitó EU remélte. Fontos különbség, hogy Tusknak Magyarral ellentétben nem volt alkotmányos többsége, a lengyel államfő pedig vétózhat, és többször meg is tette.

stories.summary: A TV2 elnök-vezérigazgatója a BIF Nyrt. auditbizottságából is távozik.

stories.title: Aki kérelmezte, aki aláírta és aki megúszta – bemutatjuk a kegyelmi ügy szereplőit
stories.summary: A mellékszereplővé vált pedofil igazgatótól a K. Endrével egy napon kegyelmet kapó, terrorizmusért elítélt Budaházy György ügyvédjéig mindenki, akinek a rendelkezésre álló iratok alapján köze lehetett a magyar közéletet bő két éve felrázó botrányhoz.

stories.title: Hoki-vb: átgázolt a briteken, nagy lépést tett Magyarország a bentmaradás felé
stories.summary: A magyar férfi jégkorong-válogatott harmadik mérkőzésén megszerezte első győzelmét az A csoportos világbajnokságon. A britek ellen az első harmadban kétszer is betalált a csapat, ez megadta az alaphangot. A bentmaradásért vívott küzdelem a legfontosabb meccsét magabiztosan, 5–0-ra nyerte meg a csapat.

stories.title: Sweden orders 4 frigates from French company Naval Group
stories.summary: Sweden plans to acquire four new frigates starting in 2030 in a deal worth billions. They will be the largest surface warships in Sweden's navy. The newest NATO member is rushing to build up its military capabilities.

stories.title: Young Czechs drink less beer but risk other addictions
stories.summary: The Czech Republic, the beer world champion, has seen a record decline in consumption. Younger generations are drinking less. Breweries are worried, but health experts say it's good news.

stories.title: Could Romania and Moldova reunite?
stories.summary: Moldovan leader Maia Sandu and her Romanian counterpart Nicusor Dan want to reunite their countries. Is that a realistic scenario?

stories.title: EU closes in on migrant returns deal
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The EU is closing in on a plan designed to increase the number of failed asylum seekers leaving the bloc. Described by Migration Commissioner Magnus Brunner as the “missing piece” of plans to toughen up EU migration policy, negotiators for the Council, Parliament and Commission are expected to reach a deal in a final round of talks on Wednesday. The rate of failed asylum seekers leaving the EU is around 20 percent, according to numbers cited by the European Commission , although

stories.title: EU strikes deal on Trump trade pact
stories.summary: STRASBOURG — The European Union agreed early Wednesday morning to implement a trade deal with the United States, likely averting a threat by President Donald Trump to punish further delay with oppressive tariffs. Negotiators from the European Parliament, the Council of the EU and the European Commission reached a compromise after more than five hours of talks on legislation to enact the accord struck last summer at Trump’s golf resort in Turnberry, Scotland. Under that pact, the EU agreed to scr

stories.title: Israeli strikes on Lebanon kill at least 19 as clashes with Hezbollah continue despite ceasefire
stories.summary: Single strike on the village of Deir Qanoun al Nahr in the coastal Tyre province killed 10, including three children and three women, health ministry says Israeli airstrikes on southern Lebanon on Tuesday killed at least 19 people, including four women and three children, Lebanon’s health ministry said, the latest in near-daily attacks from both sides that have not stopped despite a fragile, US-brokered ceasefire . Israel’s military did not immediately comment on the casualties or specific incid

stories.title: EU agrees to implement US trade deal struck last summer
stories.summary: MEPs had twice frozen ratification process in protest at Trump’s threats to increase tariffs and take control of Greenland The EU has finally agreed to implement its trade deal with the US after five hours of talks between members of the European parliament and member states in the hope of averting more tariffs threatened by Donald Trump. It means the agreement struck last July at the US president’s Scottish golf course can now enter into force, removing import duties on most US goods entering t

stories.title: Thailand tightens visa rules for tourists, citing crime by foreigners
stories.summary: Move brings an end to a 60 day visa-free stay that was agreed with 93 countries, including the UK, US and much of Europe Thailand is drastically cutting the length of visa-free stays for tourists from more than 90 countries in an effort to curb crime involving foreign nationals, officials said on Tuesday. Tourism is a crucial source of jobs in Thailand, and the country has struggled to return to the number of foreign arrivals recorded before the Covid pandemic. However, concerns over visitors br

stories.title: Did Trump really rescue Venezuela? – podcast
stories.summary: Tom Phillips on life in the country four months after the US abduction of the former president Nicolás Maduro “The last time I flew out of Venezuela was right at the start of August 2024, just after the disputed presidential election,” the Guardian’s Latin America correspondent, Tom Phillips , tells Annie Kelly . “It was a moment of real turmoil. There was a huge wave of repression that was unfolding as Nicolás Maduro tried to silence any kind of dissent to his bogus claim to have won that elect

stories.title: Britain’s second most senior diplomat in Washington abruptly leaves post
stories.summary: James Roscoe had served as deputy ambassador to US since 2022 and stood in after Peter Mandelson’s departure Britain’s second most senior diplomat in Washington, who stood in as interim ambassador after the sacking of Peter Mandelson, has abruptly left his post. The UK government gave no reason for James Roscoe’s sudden departure, which comes amid an investigation into the leak of discussions at a meeting of the UK’s national security council. Continue reading...

stories.title: UK relaxes strict sanctions on Russian crude as oil costs soar
stories.summary: Decision to allow import of jet fuel and diesel refined in third countries is criticised by Tories as ‘insane’ The UK government has relaxed strict sanctions on Russian crude oil, allowing for the import of jet fuel and diesel refined in third countries amid surging costs. A trade licence that came into effect on Wednesday permits the imports indefinitely and will be reviewed periodically. It comes at a time of growing concerns over the supply of certain fuels owing to the de facto blockade of t

stories.title: Rachel Reeves to protect ‘critical’ clean energy projects from legal challenges
stories.summary: Chancellor’s planning shake-up would ‘reduce exposure from judicial review on all but human rights grounds’ Rachel Reeves is ​preparing to announce a planning shake-up ‌that would fast-track clean energy ​and infrastructure projects by curbing judicial reviews, the ​Treasury said. The chancellor will propose that parliament should be able to designate and approve the most important clean energy projects as of “critical national importance”, as part of a wider package seeking to blunt the impact

stories.summary: ‘China has already eaten much of German industry’s lunch and is preparing to start on dinner,’ thinktank says Business live – latest updates Germany must stop admiring China’s success in the EU or it will sleepwalk into the kind of deindustrialisation the US experienced 25 years ago, a leading Brussels thinktank has said. stories.title: As WHO sounds alarm over Ebola in DRC, what can be learned from previous outbreaks?
stories.summary: Conflict, mistrust and delayed detection could complicate response to emergency caused by Bundibugyo variant To be around the centre of an Ebola outbreak is to become used to the smell of chlorine. At hospitals and government buildings, surfaces are sprayed with it and hands washed in a 0.05% solution that can kill the virus in 60 seconds. Infrared handheld thermometers take temperatures at airports and border crossings. Any indication of a fever prevents passage. Contact-tracing teams crisscros

stories.title: Strike near UAE reactor revives concerns over nuclear plant safety in wartime
stories.summary: Attack marks first time military action has forced a fully operating nuclear power plant to rely on backup generators • Middle East crisis – live updates A drone strike that cut off external power to a nuclear reactor in the United Arab Emirates this week has revived concerns over the safety of nuclear plants during wartime. Reactor no 3 at the Barakah nuclear plant lost vital off-site power for about 24 hours after the attack on Sunday, forcing it to rely on emergency diesel generators. Continu

stories.title: US Senate advances measure to limit Trump's Iran war powers
stories.summary: Louisiana Senator Bill Cassidy joined three other Republican lawmakers in supporting the measure. Though symbolic and unlikely to become law, advancing the resolution is a sign of growing frustration with the war.

stories.title: EU strikes deal on US trade pact ahead of Trump tariff deadline
stories.summary: The EU reached a provisional agreement to remove import duties on US goods, keeping the bloc on track to meet Trump's deadline to avoid higher tariffs on European exports.

stories.title: Taiwan's Lai rejects China pressure, pledges stronger defense
stories.summary: Lai Ching-te defended Taiwan's sovereignty after US President Donald Trump suggested that arms sales to the island could be used as leverage in talks with China.

stories.summary: Xi Jinping welcomed Russian President Vladimir Putin with a military ceremony in Beijing.

stories.title: Nigeria arrests former minister in hiding after corruption conviction

stories.title: Anti-government demonstrators and police clash in Bolivia
stories.summary: Protesters have been calling for the president to resign after weeks-long unrest.

stories.title: France’s most unpopular president eyes a comeback
stories.summary: PARIS — François Hollande ended his only presidential term in 2017 just months after netting a dismal 4 percent approval rating, and his time in office is now best remembered abroad for his notorious adulterous scooter trip to conduct a croissant-fueled affair with an actress. But the 71-year-old Socialist — nicknamed “Flamby” by his critics, after a wobbly custard dessert — is now eyeing a long-shot ride back to the Elysée. Hollande returned to frontline politics in 2024 by winning a seat in pa

stories.title: Donald Trump’s Republican revenge tour is complete
stories.summary: President Donald Trump finished his retribution tour with a bang. Trump ousted GOP Rep. Thomas Massie  in Kentucky  and blocked Secretary of State Brad Raffensperger’s  bid for Georgia governor  on Tuesday, capping off a monthlong revenge campaign that saw the president force out most of the Indiana state lawmakers who opposed his redistricting efforts and unseat Louisiana GOP Sen. Bill Cassidy. The show of dominance reaffirmed Trump’s iron grip on the Republican Party base e

stories.title: Vladimir Putin arrives in Beijing for state visit hot on heels of Trump
stories.summary: Russian president is welcomed to Beijing with an honour guard after saying relations with China have reached an ‘unprecedented level’ Vladimir Putin has arrived in Beijing for a state visit, four days after Donald Trump left China. Putin was greeted by China’s foreign minister, Wang Yi, when he landed on Tuesday evening, with an honour guard alongside Chinese youths waving China and Russia’s national flags in a welcome ceremony on the tarmac. stories.summary: Millions of litres of sewage have been spilling into the capital city’s waters since February after the catastrophic failure of a Moa Point wastewater plant A fix to stop millions of litres of sewage continuing to pour into the waters off the coast of New Zealand’s capital, Wellington will be in place by November, officials have said, with full repairs at the cost of NZ$53.5m by late next year. stories.summary: Keisha Lance Bottoms wins Democratic primary outright, while Republicans Burt Jones and Rick Jackson will face off The Republican primary campaign for Georgia governor will go to a June runoff, with the lieutenant governor Burt Jones facing off against healthcare billionaire Rick Jackson – and locking out Brad Raffensperger, the Georgia secretary of state and longtime political enemy of Donald Trump who was on track to finish a distant third. stories.title: Melbourne teenager Bianca Adler becomes youngest Australian to climb Mount Everest
stories.summary: The 18-year-old high school student reached the top of the world’s tallest mountain on her second attempt An 18-year-old high school student from Melbourne became the youngest Australian to climb to the top of Mount Everest on Wednesday. According to her Garmin data and a post on Instagram, Bianca Adler reached the 8,849 metre summit at nearly 6.30am Melbourne time, and nearly 2.30am Nepal time, with her guides, Pemba and Ngdu. Continue reading...

stories.title: Limit social media ban for under-16s to unsafe apps, Starmer urged
stories.summary: Campaigners warn against blanket restrictions and say focus should be on blocking teenagers from platforms with ‘risky’ features Online safety campaigners have urged Keir Starmer to block under-16s from accessing social media apps that do not meet strict safety standards, instead of implementing a broader Australia-style ban. stories.title: Teenagers behind mass shooting in San Diego rushed at mosque ‘fully armored’
stories.summary: Firearms used by shooters, aged 17 and 18, in fatal rampage were registered to one of their parents The two teenage assailants responsible for a mass shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego, California, rushed toward the mosque “fully armored” with handguns and rifles, authorities said. A security guard shot and struck one of the shooters, according to members of the mosque, but the attacker continued charging. The guard, Amin Abdullah, alerted administrators of the school at the Islamic Cen

stories.title: US claims ‘emergency refugee situation’ as it admits 10,000 more white South Africans
stories.summary: Trump has repeatedly made false claims that white Afrikaners facing genocide with costs of resettling them at $100m The US government has said it will increase the number of white South Africans it admits as refugees this year from about 7,500 to 17,500, claiming that “unforeseen developments in South Africa created an emergency refugee situation.” Since starting his second term in office last year, Donald Trump has repeatedly made false claims that white Afrikaners are racially targeted and fac

stories.title: Woman shot dead by police in Jamaica at protest over previous police shooting
stories.summary: Investigation launched as video circulates online showing officer firing at vehicle and colleagues dragging away body Authorities in Jamaica have launched an investigation after CCTV footage of a woman’s fatal shooting by police sent shock waves across the Caribbean nation. Footage circulating on social media shows a police officer firing at a vehicle during a protest on Sunday in Granville, in Jamaica’s north-western parish of St James. The bullet hit Latoya Bulgin, 45, who was behind the wheel

stories.title: Trump threatens ‘a big hit’ if Tehran does not make deal soon
stories.summary: Renewed threat comes after US president said he was ‘an hour away’ from ordering a strike before pulling back Middle East crisis – live updates Donald Trump has again threatened Iran, saying the US may launch new attacks if Tehran continues to refuse the significant concessions he wants before a deal can be struck to end the Middle East war. The US president said he had called off a fresh wave of strikes , which would have broken the ceasefire in place since early last month. “I was an hour away

stories.title: US to reduce troops in Europe to 2021 levels, Pentagon says
stories.summary: The scaling back would bring the number of US troops on the continent to the level before Russia launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine. The Pentagon said that was in line with Trump's 'America first' agenda.

stories.title: Russian President Vladimir Putin in Beijing for talks with Chinese leader Xi Jinping
stories.summary: Presidents Xi Jinping and Vladimir Putin each praised their countries' bilateral relations during the Russian leader's visit to Beijing. Follow DW for more.

stories.title: Millions follow India's viral 'cockroach' movement
stories.summary: What started as an internet joke is fast becoming young India's newest symbol of resilience, survival and collective frustration. Meanwhile, PM Modi has reached Italy for the last leg of his tour. DW has more.

stories.title: EU is failing to cut pesticide use
stories.summary: With binding cut targets scrapped, the European Union's pledge to halve pesticide use is stalling — even as controversial chemicals like glyphosate remain on sale across the bloc.

stories.title: Germany news: Ebola patient admitted to Berlin hospital
stories.summary: An American doctor infected with the Ebola virus has been flown to Berlin's Charité hospital for specialist treatment. Meanwhile, three people remain missing in Görlitz following a house collapse. DW has more.

stories.title: Meta's pre-teen profile on Insta: How an AI will scan images of birthday cakes and bone structure to remove users under 13 years of age
stories.summary: Instagram and Facebook have a problem with underage users — there's too many of them. Parent company, Meta, aims to root out under-13s with an age-profiling AI. This plays into a debate about a teenage social media ban.

stories.title: Iran war: Why is Trump blaming the Kurds?
stories.summary: Donald Trump says Kurdish forces kept US weapons meant for Iranian protesters, allegations Kurdish leaders categorically deny. Analysts say the president is scapegoating the groups to make up for his own policy failures.

stories.title: Germany beefs up civil defense
stories.summary: Germany's government wants to spend €10 billion to better protect its population in case of emergencies. The backlog is huge.

stories.title: Rising Cuba-US tensions amid talk of drones, emergency plans
stories.summary: Cuba’s reported purchase of drones and renewed threats with the US are stoking fears of escalation. Amid blackouts and shortages, many Cubans expressed outrage at official advice to prepare for potential attack.

stories.title: Father-of-eight killed in San Diego mosque shooting hailed as hero
stories.summary: Security guard Amin Abdullah, one of three killed in the attack, was described as "a shining light".

stories.title: Trump endorses Paxton in Texas, gambling on a challenger with baggage in a crucial race
stories.summary: Trump's move dismayed Senate Republicans, many of whom have served with the Texan for decades.

stories.summary: On Wednesday, Putin said he had held "substantive" talks with Beijing on bilateral cooperation.

stories.summary: A Tripoli appeals court has acquitted 31 former Gaddafi regime officials of suppressing the 2011 revolution, closing a case that resulted in multiple death sentences in 2015.

stories.title: What is Ebola and why is stopping the latest outbreak so difficult?

stories.summary: Both Democratic and Republican lawmakers have questioned whether the military has the legal authority to use lethal force against civilian-style vessels in international waters outside of a declared theatre of war.

The European Commission has been asked about its response to the renewed talk about Britain’s potential future attempt to rejoin the European Union – but did not take a bait. At this stage, there are discussions on closer cooperation in a number of areas, that’s where we are, and that’s what we are doing precisely in prepa

stories.title: Pressure on Mexico after two ex-officials surrender to US over alleged cartel ties
stories.summary: Mexican president Claudia Sheinbaum denies any links between her Morena party and organized crime Pressure is mounting on Claudia Sheinbaum, Mexico’s president, after two former top officials from the country’s Sinaloa state – both members of her Morena party – gave themselves up to US authorities over alleged ties to the Sinaloa cartel. Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus sai

stories.title: Rubio criticizes WHO’s Ebola response as US continues sweeping public health cuts
stories.summary: US secretary of state says WHO was ‘a little late’ in identifying deadly Ebola outbreak in the DRC and Uganda The US secretary of state, Marco Rubio, said on Tuesday that the World Health Organization (WHO) was “a little late” in identifying the deadly Ebola outbreak in the the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda. In Trump’s telling, the “dealmaker-in-chief” has maintained a consistent policy toward Iran aimed at preventing Tehran from

stories.title: Hearing aids may cut noise by reading brain signals
stories.summary: In a crowded room, it can be impossible for hearing aid users to focus on what they want or need to hear. Research into auditory neurotechnology may enhance sounds and voices and reduce noise in real-time.

stories.title: China's Africa trade push: Partnership or power grab?
stories.summary: China has opened its market to 53 African countries, calling it a "golden key" to prosperity. But while new opportunities emerge, analysts warn that the reality for African economies is more complex.

stories.summary: Authorities say the teenage suspects shared a "broad hatred" of multiple religions and racial groups.

stories.summary: Egyikük egy apró baranyai faluból indult, ma már mesterképzésre jár és gyógytornászként dolgozik. Másikuk hamarosan diplomát szerez, miközben JYSK-mentorával rendszeresen a karrierépítésről és a jövőbeni lehetőségekről beszélget. A lakberendezési áruházlánc szerint a társadalmi mobilitás nemcsak pénzügyi támogatáson múlik: legalább ennyire fontos az is, hogy legyen valaki, aki figyel, kérdez, segít és hosszú távon is támogat. Olyan, akire számítani lehet.

stories.title: A pénzt sem sajnálják az ügyfelek számláiért harcoló bankok

Ezzel is azt mutatják, hogy nem ölbe tett kézzel várják a Revolut itteni startját. A BiztosDöntés.hu összesítése szerint a Gránit Bank teljesen új számlacsomagok bevezetésével indította a májust, az OTP is látványos lépéseket tett, és új, kibővített jóváírási akciókkal is megjelentek a bankok.

stories.title: Could the UK really rejoin the EU? - The Latest
stories.summary: The Brexit debate has been reignited after Labour leadership contender Wes Streeting called it a ‘catastrophic mistake’ and said the UK should rejoin the European Union. His comments put pressure on rival Andy Burnham, who has previously advocated for rejoining the bloc but is fighting a byelection in the leave-voting Makerfield constituency. But how would rejoining work and would the EU even agree to it? Lucy Hough speaks to the Guardian’s Europe correspondent, Jon Henley Continue reading...

When Donald Trump met Xi Jinping in Beijing on Thursday, many feared the unpredictable US leader could upend Washington’s longstanding support for Taipei. But beyond a starkly worded statement from Xi stressing China’s claims over Taiwan, which it claims as part of its territory despite never having ruled it, in

stories.title: How China became Russia's economic lifeline
stories.summary: Hot on the heels of Donald Trump's visit, Russian President Vladimir Putin travels to Beijing for talks with Xi Jinping. DW explores how China's trade and financial support is helping keep Russia's economy afloat.

stories.title: Fires and damaged buildings after massive Russian drone attack

stories.title: Iran calls for reconstruction support at World Urban Forum in Baku

stories.title: Fears of new China shock as EU industry’s reliance on imports grows
stories.summary: Rising volume of components imported from China prompts warning of cannibalisation of European industries Europe is facing a fresh China shock that threatens to cannibalise local factories, leading to job losses and de facto colonisation of industry by Beijing, trade analysts and representatives have said. They fear the plunging exchange rate and support for Chinese “zombie firms” has echoes of the crisis in the US 25 years ago when the term “China shock” was coined. It referred to the impact of

stories.title: Calls for release of Sierra Leonean singer jailed in ‘crackdown on free speech’
stories.summary: Zainab Sheriff unjustly sentenced to four years in prison for incitement and threatening language, say activists Lawyers, politicians and activists have called for the release of one of Sierra Leone’s best-known celebrities, who they said was unjustly imprisoned as part of a government crackdown on free speech and political dissent. Zainab Sheriff, a singer and reality-TV show contestant who became a political opposition figure, was sentenced in April to four years and two months’ imprisonment f

stories.title: Trump bugyuta háborúja, ami csak az oroszoknak jó
stories.summary: Miért szükséges az USA-nak és Iránnak is, hogy egy éven belül lezáruljon az iráni háború? Miért érzi mindkét fél előnyösnek a mostani pozícióját? A Téma új adásában az iráni háború hatásairól és kilátásairól beszélgettünk.

stories.title: Miért került szóba megint Budaházy ügye? Varga ellenezte, Novák sorra megkegyelmezett a Hunnia-per szereplőinek
stories.summary: Többféle kegyelemmel is élt Novák Katalin az évek során a Hunnia-perben, amiben végül a terrorizmusért elítélt Budaházy György lóháton hagyhatta el a váci börtönt.

stories.title: Levideózta ellenfelei edzéseit a Southampton, kizárták a focicsapatot az angol másodosztály rájátszásából
stories.summary: A Southampton is elismerte a szabálysértést, de fellebbeznek, mert szerintük aránytalan a büntetés.

stories.title: Döntetlenezett a Manchester City, 22 év után angol bajnok az Arsenal
stories.summary: Kifogott a Bournemouth Guardiolán, az Arsenal 2004 után újra bajnok.

stories.title: Balhés közgyűlést tartott a kerékpáros szövetség, egyelőre nem döntöttek az elnökség leváltásáról
stories.summary: Kevés az eredmény és a pénz, jelenleg az olimpiai kvótaszerzés is távolinak tűnik az országúti szakágban a férfiaknál.

stories.title: A mandalóri és Grogu csak egy mellékküldetés, nem pedig mozifilm
stories.summary: A mandalóri és Grogu a bizonyíték arra, hogy a Disney ennyi év után még mindig nem tudja, mihez kezdjen a Star Warsszal. Nem azért, mert A mandalóri és Grogu (innentől M&G) kifejezetten rossz film lenne, vagy mert ne éreztem volna magam egész jól a vetítés alatt. Ami a felszínen egy könnyed kaland a messzi, messzi galaxis peremvidékén, az valójában a háttérben egy komoly iparági identitásválság. Kritika A mandalóri és Grogu című filmről.

stories.title: Elversberg: Bundesliga newcomers from tiny town make history
stories.summary: A team from a small south-western German town of just 7,000 inhabitants has made history by sealing promotion to become the 59th club to play in the Bundesliga.

stories.title: Germany's crisis-hit chemical industry seeks revival
stories.summary: Hit by high energy costs and regulation, Germany's chemical companies are scaling back production at home while expanding abroad. Can the trend be reversed?

stories.title: Germany news: 2030 climate goals in danger, experts warn
stories.summary: Headlines in Germany this Monday include higher than planned CO2 emissions, plans to ramp up civil defense measures, and Chancellor Merz meeting euroskeptic Bulgarian Premier Rumen Radev.

stories.title: Cats in costumes and beauty contests draw crowds at expo
stories.summary: Thousands of cat lovers gathered at Cat Expo India in Mumbai, where visitors explored cat beauty contests, pet care products, grooming services, accessories and premium food brands alongside dozens of felines.

stories.title: Two men arrested over stunt at enclosure of famous monkey Punch
stories.summary: Two US nationals have been arrested after one jumped the fence of the monkey, who went viral online, police say.

stories.title: Moment two fighter jets collide mid-air during US air show
stories.summary: Two fighter jets collided mid-air during an air show in Idaho, sending both aircraft crashing to the ground as crew members parachuted to safety.

stories.title: Iran sets up Hormuz transit authority to charge ships for passage
stories.summary: Tehran has launched a formal body to manage and charge ships for transit through the Strait of Hormuz in a highly-contested move as tankers pile up in the waterway and peace talks remain deadlocked.

stories.title: Death toll from Israeli strikes on Lebanon passes 3,000, officials say
stories.summary: It marks a grim milestone in the conflict between Israel and the Lebanese armed group Hezbollah since March, despite a nominal ceasefire.

stories.title: 'I sold it for over £1k': Swatch launch sparks 'chaotic' scenes
stories.summary: Some shoppers have been trying to resell a new line of watches priced at £335 each for up to £16,000.

stories.title: Four killed in protests during Kenyan strikes over high fuel prices
stories.summary: Thousands of commuters are stranded, with key roads in the capital Nairobi largely empty due to the protests over fuel costs.

stories.title: Belarus stages nuclear drills with Russia as Kyiv warns of new offensive
stories.summary: Russia and Belarus conducted joint nuclear exercises on Monday as Kyiv warned Moscow was preparing a new offensive from Belarusian territory against Ukraine or NATO.

stories.title: 'No society is immune' to housing crisis, UN chief warns at World Urban Forum in Baku
stories.summary: António Guterres told the World Urban Forum in Baku that no country is immune from the housing crisis, as 40,000 delegates gathered for the second day of talks.

stories.title: Trump administration unveils $1.8bn fund to compensate his allies as he drops tax lawsuit
stories.summary: The settlement will go towards individuals who allege they were subjected to unfair investigations.

stories.title: Trump: I called off attack on Iran planned for Tuesday
stories.summary: The US president said he had been asked to do so by the leaders of Qatar, Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates as "serious negotiations are now taking place."

stories.title: The Republican who stood up to Trump is hoping for a big win against president
stories.summary: Kentucky Congressman Thomas Massie, who has crossed Trump on federal spending and the Epstein files, faces Republican voters.

stories.title: Musk loses OpenAI court battle after jury finds he waited too long to sue
stories.summary: Jurors spent weeks hearing about Musk's claim that Altman had "stolen a charity."

stories.title: Trump says he called off new Iran attack at request of Gulf states
stories.summary: The US president says he is holding off on an attack planned for Tuesday as "serious negotiations are now taking place".

stories.title: What really holds China and Russia together
stories.summary: The relationship endures despite a power imbalance, with both sides recognising that it is too important to fail.

stories.title: Trump told Taiwan not to 'go independent' - but does it want to?
stories.summary: Taiwan independence is the reddest of red lines for China, which claims the island as its own.

stories.title: Three people killed as two teenagers open fire at a mosque in San Diego
stories.summary: Police say the two suspects had exhibited behaviour indicating a “generalised hate rhetoric” towards Muslims. The attack is one of the most major to target the Californian city’s large Muslim community.

stories.title: Starbucks Korea sacks CEO over controversial 'Tank Day' promotion
stories.summary: Starbucks withdrew the campaign for its drink tumblers after many said it referenced a bloody crackdown.

stories.title: Fast-moving wildfire spreads through Southern California hills
stories.summary: A fast-moving brush fire in Simi Valley, Ventura County, has forced evacuations and damaged homes as it spreads across more than 180 acres in California.

stories.title: Swatch fans left frustrated as stores stay closed amid watch frenzy
stories.summary: Dozens of shoppers gathered outside a Swatch store in Lyon hoping to buy a sought-after watch released with Audemars Piguet, only to find the shop closed following disturbances and stock shortages.

stories.title: Europol disrupts thousands of IRGC online accounts across 19 countries
stories.summary: Europol announced a major digital crackdown that led to the removal of thousands of online accounts linked to Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) and the suspension of the group's primary X account in the EU.

stories.title: Tiger escapes enclosure near Leipzig, one person injured
stories.summary: An escaped tiger has injured a 73-year-old man near Leipzig, with police saying they shot the animal. German media reported the tiger was owned by a controversial performer.

stories.title: Spain has banned Francoist symbols. So why are there still kitsch cafes glorifying the dictator? | Abbas Asaria
stories.summary: These unsettling and unusual places tell a vivid story about the unique way that Spain deals with its past – or fails to Una Grande Libre reads the sign above the entrance to a bar-restaurant in Madrid’s Usera neighbourhood. This was Francisco Franco’s motto for Spain – one, great, free – and it is accompanied by a large portrait of the dictator superimposed on to the window.
